description:
Goal: execute compiler2 macro functions as compile-time Fz code over process-heap quoted AST values, without the old `CompileTimeEvaluator` or `exec::Value` AST codec.

Signal:
- a local `defmacro inc(x) do quote do: unquote(x) + 1 end end` can run during source production and return quoted AST
- macro functions receive quoted args and caller env in a shape compatible with `__CALLER__`
- quote/unquote allocate and splice process-heap source values, preserving lexical context metadata
- macro execution errors surface through compiler2 diagnostics/telemetry with call-site and definition context
- no fz2 path calls `frontend::macros::expand_program_with_types`, `exec::ast_value`, or the scratch `CompileTimeEvaluator`

Change:
- add compile-time process/interpreter entry points for macro functions defined by compiler2 services
- implement quote/unquote over the compiler2 quoted source value model
- define deterministic macro execution limits/signals for recursion/runaway expansion
- add telemetry for macro execution start/stop/error carrying raw ids/source facts, not formatted strings

Why:
- the old evaluator proves the semantics, but its value model and whole-program preload are the wrong substrate
- macros are ordinary compiled compile-time functions; their only authority is returned source data and compiler-service calls
title: Compiler2 macro runtime: execute defmacro functions on a compile-time process heap
